title: Charmed Life — 3 (Fragment 2)
sourceText:
“I’m taking you in this way,” Mr. Saunders explained cheerfully, “because it’s the door you’ll be using mostly, and I thought it would help you find your way about if you start as you mean to go on.”

Cat did not mind. He thought this door looked more homely. But Gwendolen, cheated of her grand entry, threw Mr. Saunders a seething look and wondered whether to say a most unpleasant spell at him. She decided against it. She was still wanting to give a good impression. They got out of the car and followed Mr. Saunders—whose coat had a way of billowing even when there was no wind—into a square polished passageway indoors. A most imposing lady was waiting there to meet them. She was wearing a tight purple dress and her hair was in a very tall jet-black pile. Cat thought she must be Mrs. Chrestomanci.

“This is Miss Bessemer, the housekeeper,” said Mr. Saunders. “Eric and Gwendolen, Miss Bessemer. Eric’s a bit carsick, I’m afraid.”

Cat had not realized his trouble was so obvious. He was embarrassed. Gwendolen, who was very annoyed to be met by a mere housekeeper, held her hand out coldly to Miss Bessemer.

Miss Bessemer shook hands like an Empress. Cat was just thinking she was the most awe-inspiring lady he had ever met when she turned to him with a very kind smile. “Poor Eric,” she said. “Riding in a car bothers me ever so too. You’ll be all right now you’re out of the thing—but if you’re not, I’ll give you something for it. Come and get washed, and have a look-see at your rooms.”

They followed the narrow purple triangle of her dress up some stairs, along corridors, and up more stairs. Cat had never seen anywhere so luxurious. There was carpet the whole way—a soft green carpet, like grass in the dewy morning—and the floor at the sides was polished so that it reflected the carpet and the clean white walls and the pictures hung on the walls. Everywhere was very quiet. They heard nothing the whole way, except their own feet and Miss Bessemer’s purple rustle.

Miss Bessemer opened a door onto a blaze of afternoon sun. “This is your room, Gwendolen. Your bathroom opens off it.”

“Thank you,” said Gwendolen, and she sailed magnificently in to take possession of it. Cat peeped past Miss Bessemer and saw the room was very big, with a rich, soft Turkey carpet covering most of the floor.

Miss Bessemer said, “The Family dines early when there are no visitors so that they can eat with the children. But I expect you’d like some tea all the same. Whose room shall I have it sent to?”

“Mine, please,” Gwendolen said at once.

There was a short pause before Miss Bessemer said, “Well, that’s settled then, isn’t it? Your room is up here, Eric.”

The way was up a twisting staircase. Cat was pleased. It looked as if his room was going to be part of the old castle. And he was right. When Miss Bessemer opened the door, the room beyond was round, and the three windows showed that the wall was nearly three feet thick. Cat could not resist racing across the glowing carpet to scramble on one of the deep window seats and look out. He found he could see across the flat tops of the cedars to a great lawn like a sheet of green velvet, with flower gardens going down the hillside in steps beyond it. Then he looked around the room itself. The curved walls were whitewashed, and so was the thick fireplace. The bed had a patchwork quilt on it. There was a table, a chest-of-drawers, and a bookcase with interesting-looking books in it.

words.definition: In a happy and optimistic manner.
words.example: Mr. Saunders explained cheerfully.
words.exampleText: Mr. Saunders explained cheerfully.

words.definition: Simple but cosy and comfortable, like home.
words.example: This door looked more homely.
words.exampleText: Cat thought this door looked more homely.

words.definition: Extremely angry but trying to keep silent.
words.example: Gwendolen, cheated of her grand entry, threw Mr. Saunders a seething look.
words.exampleText: She threw a seething look at Mr. Saunders.

words.definition: Grand and impressive in appearance.
words.example: A most imposing lady was waiting there to meet them.
words.exampleText: A most imposing lady was waiting there.

words.definition: Swelling out or puffing up, especially with wind.
words.example: His coat had a way of billowing even when there was no wind.
words.exampleText: The coat was billowing even without wind.

words.definition: The area near the sea, often for holidays or leisure.
words.example: They walked along the seaside.

words.definition: Causing a feeling of respect mixed with fear or wonder.
words.example: She was the most awe-inspiring lady he had ever met.
words.exampleText: Miss Bessemer was awe-inspiring.

words.definition: Causes a person to feel troubled or uneasy.
words.example: Riding in a car bothers me ever so too.
words.exampleText: Car rides bother Miss Bessemer.

words.definition: Extremely comfortable, elegant, or enjoyable, often involving great expense.
words.example: The house was very luxurious.
words.exampleText: The rooms seemed luxurious.

words.definition: Covered with dew; fresh and moist.
words.example: Grass in the dewy morning.
words.exampleText: The carpet was like grass in the dewy morning.

words.definition: Made smooth and shiny by rubbing; refined or elegant.
words.example: The polished floor reflected the carpet.
words.exampleText: The floor was polished and shiny.

words.definition: A soft, muffled crackling sound like leaves or clothes moving.
words.example: Miss Bessemer’s purple rustle could be heard.
words.exampleText: They heard Miss Bessemer’s rustle.

words.definition: The state of having or owning something.
words.example: She sailed magnificently in to take possession of it.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen took possession of the room.

words.definition: An idea or opinion of something or someone, especially one formed without conscious thought.
words.example: She was still wanting to give a good impression.
words.exampleText: She wanted to give a good impression.

words.definition: Feeling self-conscious or awkward.
words.example: Cat was embarrassed by his trouble.
words.exampleText: He was embarrassed to be carsick.

words.definition: A feeling of slight anger or displeasure.
words.example: Gwendolen was annoyed at being met by a mere housekeeper.
words.exampleText: She was annoyed by the situation.

words.definition: Used to emphasize how small or insignificant someone or something is.
words.example: Met by a mere housekeeper.
words.exampleText: She was met by a mere housekeeper.

words.definition: A shape with three sides and three angles; metaphorically a shape or pattern.
words.example: The narrow purple triangle of her dress.
words.exampleText: Her dress had a purple triangle shape.

words.definition: To move smoothly and effortlessly, sometimes used figuratively.
words.example: She sailed magnificently in to take possession.
words.exampleText: She sailed into the room.

words.definition: Turning or winding repeatedly in different directions.
words.example: The way was up a twisting staircase.
words.exampleText: The twisting staircase was narrow.

words.definition: To move quickly using both hands and feet, often in an awkward or hurried way.
words.example: Cat scrambled on one of the deep window seats.
words.exampleText: He scrambled onto the window seat.

words.definition: The side of a hill.
words.example: Flower gardens going down the hillside in steps.
words.exampleText: Gardens stepped down the hillside.

words.definition: Covered with a white paint-like substance, typically lime.
words.example: The curved walls were whitewashed.
words.exampleText: Walls were whitewashed white.

words.definition: Something made up of many different pieces or parts sewn together.
words.example: A patchwork quilt on the bed.
words.exampleText: The bed had a patchwork quilt.

words.definition: A broad flat area or layer of something.
words.example: A great lawn like a sheet of green velvet.
words.exampleText: The lawn was like a sheet of velvet.

words.definition: A tall mass or heap, here used for hairstyle meaning a high hairstyle or hairdo.
words.example: Her hair was in a very tall jet-black pile.
words.exampleText: Her hair was a tall jet-black pile.

words.definition: Having qualities considered typical of a lady, refined and polite.
words.example: She shook hands like an Empress, ladylike and dignified.

words.definition: Having or owning something, control or occupancy of property.
words.example: She sailed magnificently in to take possession of it.
